creator: raimeetravel
caption:
How I transfer my points to airline partners (you all asked for a tutorial!!!):

✨ Step 1: Find the flight FIRST
I always start on seats.aero because I have multiple cards + programs and I want to see all my options in one place. This shows me which airline program has the best deal.

✨ Step 2: Go to my credit card portal
Once I find a flight I like, I log into my card portal → click “transfer to travel partners”

✨ Step 3: Double check availability
THIS is the step people skip ❗️
I go to the airline’s actual website and make sure:
– the flight is there
– it’s the same points amount
– it’s actually bookable

✨ Step 4: Transfer points
Only after confirming, I transfer my points to that airline partner.

Most transfers are instant (like ~10 min), but some can take longer.

✨ Step 5: Book immediately
As soon as the points hit, I book directly through the airline’s site.

💡 Why this matters:
Booking through airline partners can get you WAY better value than booking through your card portal. Same flight = sometimes half the points!!! Especially for flying upper class! 

Comment CARDS and I’ll send you my favorite credit cards to earn and transfer points 💳✨

#pointsandmiles #travelcards #traveltips

transcription.text: i'm going to show you my process for transferring my credit card points to book flights because airline prices are crazy now so it's a great time to learn how to best utilize your credit card points if you have them. and i just saved about seven thousand dollars because i used my points instead of paying for all the flights i need to take this year. my first step is obviously finding the flight. i use seats.arrow to search a ton of points and miles options all at once. it allows me to filter by my specific car. then i can see all my flight options and how many points it will cost me in each cabin class. then i log into my credit card portal where i have my points. i click on the rewards tab. each credit card portal is going to want you to use their specific portal to book but you shouldn't. you should go here. they always make it a little tricky to find but you'll go to travel and then transfer to partners. then you can see all of the loyalty programs and airlines and you can match it to whatever you found on seats.arrow. so i found a flight with air france. i click flying blue. i click to transfer my points and you'll have to make a loyalty account. it's free to do so if you don't have one. you put in your membership number here and you tell them how many points you want to transfer over into that account. then log into that account. you'll see the points there. search for the flight you want and book with those miles. and that's it. it's pretty easy. one thing that i do recommend you do is go to that airline website and make sure you see the flight you want to book and the points you want to use on the airline's exact website before you actually transfer the points. good luck and hopefully we can all book the flights we want to book for this year because it's crazy.
